title: 1 in 4 Americans use AI for health information, with 14 million skipping doctor visits as a result, Gallup finds
description: 14 million Americans skipped a doctor visit last month based on AI health advice, per a West Health-Gallup survey of 5,660 adults. Cost drives the trend-32% of low-income users turned to AI because they couldn't afford care.

title: Young workers need honesty about the impact of AI on jobs
description: Entry-level professionals are being celebrated for their AI skills, but no one is being frank about the fact these new tools will replace those workers.

title: AI companies are rationing compute. It could be a boon for traditional software.
T-Mobile says T-Force customer support is still handled by humans, not AI
description: T-Mobile says its T-Force social support team is still staffed by human employees, not AI. The denial came after a Reddit user spotted a post-chat survey asking if they knew they'd spoken with a "virtual agent."

title: Equinix launches Fabric Intelligence to automate network management for AI workloads
description: Equinix launched Fabric Intelligence, a platform that automates network design and management for AI workloads across data centers and cloud regions. It compresses deployment timelines from weeks to minutes using natural language commands.
